close_reason: DER parser is ~50 lines with consistent rest-passing idiom. Cursor abstraction adds indirection for no clarity gain.
description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:150-183 — der_length and der_integer manually thread '(value, rest) = ...' through split_first / split_at, returning Option<(_, &[u8])>. This is correct and reasonably idiomatic, but for a parser that grows (see F5/F6 strict-DER fixes), a small std-only Cursor-like helper, or using std::io::Cursor over &[u8], would scale better.

What goes wrong without it: when the parser grows to enforce minimal-length encoding (F5), trailing-bytes check (F6), and any future TLV extensions, the manual rest-threading style multiplies the surface area where 'forgot to update rest' is a class of bug.

Filed as opinion: the current form is fine for the current scope. Only escalate if F5/F6 actually expand the parser significantly. The der/x509 ecosystem (rustcrypto/formats der crate) has well-tested parsers that would replace this entirely; consider depending on 'der' (already in the workspace via sister crates) instead of carrying our own DER reader.
title: DER parser blends slice indexing and split_at; could use a small Cursor-style helper
